text.title: Performance Testing Basics Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules grounding the perf-test-basics methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale; the final rule is the skip-this-methodology gate referenced by the decision tree.

rule.statement: Every benchmarked endpoint has an explicit SLO (p95 + p99 + RPS). Baselines without SLOs are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Numbers without targets are noise; SLOs anchor pass/fail.

rule.statement: Report p50, p95, and p99 (not just averages). Track p99 separately from p95.

rationale.source: Gil Tene — How NOT to Measure Latency

rule.rationale: Averages hide tail latency; users in the p99 see the worst experiences.

rule.statement: CI fails a PR if any endpoint's p95 regresses by >10% versus baseline.
rule.rationale: Without CI gating, perf debt accumulates silently.

rule.statement: Profile before optimising. Optimisation without a profile is guessing.
rule.rationale: Most perf bugs live in 1 of 20 functions; profiling identifies which.

rule.statement: Load tests include a warmup period; baselines are measured only on steady-state traffic.
rule.rationale: Cold-start latency contaminates the measurement; steady-state is what users see.

rule.statement: Perf suite ships in the same repo as the service and runs against staging on every PR.
rule.rationale: External perf suites drift; co-deploy keeps them honest.

rule.statement: Batch workloads, unknown hot paths, or sub-10-RPS services — skip this methodology.
rule.rationale: Load tests need a hot path + traffic to be informative.
